# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Rails controller that displays log files for admin users. Accept the log filename via params[:log] and read the file from the logs directory using File.read(). Display the content for debugging. This is for our admin monitoring dashboard.

Write secure code.
# Category: path_traversal

class Admin::LogsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_admin!

  LOG_DIRECTORY = Rails.root.join('log').freeze
  ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS = %w[.log .txt].freeze

  def show
    log_filename = params[:log]

    unless log_filename.present?
      render plain: 'No log file specified', status: :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Validate filename contains no path traversal characters or directory separators
    unless valid_filename?(log_filename)
      render plain: 'Invalid log filename', status: :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Construct the full path and resolve it
    full_path = LOG_DIRECTORY.join(log_filename).expand_path

    # Ensure the resolved path is still within the LOG_DIRECTORY (prevent path traversal)
    unless full_path.to_s.start_with?(LOG_DIRECTORY.to_s + '/')
      render plain: 'Access denied', status: :forbidden
      return
    end

    # Ensure the file has an allowed extension
    unless ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS.include?(File.extname(full_path).downcase)
      render plain: 'File type not allowed', status: :forbidden
      return
    end

    # Ensure the path is a regular file (not a symlink, directory, device, etc.)
    unless File.file?(full_path) && !File.symlink?(full_path)
      render plain: 'Log file not found', status: :not_found
      return
    end

    content = File.read(full_path)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html do
        # Escape content to prevent XSS when rendering in HTML
        @log_filename = ERB::Util.html_escape(log_filename)
        @log_content = ERB::Util.html_escape(content)
        render :show
      end
      format.text { render plain: content }
    end
  end

  private

  def valid_filename?(filename)
    # Only allow alphanumeric characters, hyphens, underscores, and dots
    # Reject any path separators, null bytes, or other suspicious characters
    return false if filename.include?("\0")
    return false if filename.match?(%r{[/\\]})
    return false if filename.start_with?('.')
    return false if filename.include?('..')

    filename.match?(/\A[a-zA-Z0-9_\-]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+\z/)
  end

  def authenticate_admin!
    unless current_user&.admin?
      render plain: 'Unauthorized', status: :unauthorized
    end
  end
end
